APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Dana Corporation (Now Known as Dana Companies LLC)
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Victor Esposto

RULING:
DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION (NOW KNOWN AS DANA COMPANIES, LLC)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant DANA CORPORATION (NOW KNOWN AS DANA COMPANIES LLC)'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos from Victor gaskets. Defendant's separate statement did not address plaintiff's deposition testimony that he swept up materials at the Gasket Shop and there is insufficient basis to conclude that the materials that plaintiff swept up did not include asbestos-containing Victor gaskets and that such sweeping up did not expose plaintiff to respirable fibers. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The deposition testimony of Mr. Brumley and the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing due to the conduct of defendant. The objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Brumley and the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay are overruled. Order signed in open court.
